lede: A look at March 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Katsuko Saruhashi and Paul Doumer, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Comet Hale–Bopp
events.desc: Comet Hale–Bopp reaches its closest approach to Earth at 1.315 AU.

events.title: Astronaut
events.desc: Cosmonaut Valeri Polyakov returns to earth after setting a record of 438 days in space.

events.title: USAir Flight 405
events.desc: USAir Flight 405 crashes shortly after takeoff from New York City's LaGuardia Airport, leading to a number of studies into the effect that ice has on aircraft.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: In the Mediterranean Sea, the Royal Navy confronts Italy's Regia Marina in the Second Battle of Sirte.

events.title: Phan Xích Long
events.desc: Mystic Phan Xích Long, the self-proclaimed Emperor of Vietnam, is arrested for organising a revolt against the colonial rule of French Indochina, which was nevertheless carried out by his supporters the following day.

events.title: Charilaos Vasilakos
events.desc: Charilaos Vasilakos wins the first modern Olympic marathon race with a time of three hours and 18 minutes.

events.title: Emerald Buddha
events.desc: The Emerald Buddha is moved with great ceremony to its current location in Wat Phra Kaew, Thailand.

events.title: Parliament of Great Britain
events.desc: The British Parliament passes the Stamp Act that introduces a tax to be levied directly on its American colonies.

events.title: Anne Hutchinson
events.desc: Anne Hutchinson is expelled from Massachusetts Bay Colony for religious dissent.

events.title: AD 106
events.desc: Start of the Bostran era, the calendar of the province of Arabia Petraea.

births.name: Paul Doumer
births.line: French mathematician, journalist, and politician, 14th President of France

births.name: Shawn Bradley
births.line: German-American basketball player, coach, and actor

deaths.text: Pierre Clostermann, French soldier, pilot, and politician
Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, German novelist, poet, playwright, and diplomat
Dietrich of Nieheim, German bishop and historian

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Clemens August Graf von Galen
